sources.citation: Cormen et al. CLRS 4th ed. MIT Press, 2022.

learning_objectives: L1: Define time and space complexity using Big-O notation.
L1: Identify complexities for arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, hash tables, BSTs.
L1: State divide-and-conquer and name algorithms using it.
L2: Explain why merge sort is O(n log n) using a recurrence tree.
L2: Describe how a hash table achieves O(1) average-case lookup.
L2: Compare DFS and BFS by data structure, order, and use cases.
L3: Trace insertion sort, merge sort, and binary search on given input.
L3: Select an appropriate data structure and justify using complexity analysis.

concepts.title: Asymptotic Complexity
concepts.explanation: Big-O: upper bound of growth rate as n→∞. O(1) constant, O(log n) logarithmic, O(n) linear, O(n log n) linearithmic, O(n²) quadratic, O(2ⁿ) exponential. Drop constants and lower-order terms.
concepts.worked_example: Single loop: O(n). Nested loop: O(n²). Binary search: O(log n). Merge sort: O(n log n). HashMap lookup: O(1) average.

concepts.title: Arrays, Lists, Stacks, Queues
concepts.explanation: Array: O(1) index, O(n) insert/delete middle. Linked list: O(n) access, O(1) head insert/delete. Stack (LIFO): push/pop O(1). Queue (FIFO): enqueue/dequeue O(1).
concepts.worked_example: Balanced parentheses: push opens onto stack; on close, pop and check match. LIFO mirrors nesting order.

concepts.title: Hash Tables and BSTs
concepts.explanation: Hash table: O(1) average insert/delete/lookup. BST: O(log n) balanced. Use hash table for fast lookup; BST when sorted order needed.
concepts.worked_example: Word frequency counter for 1M words: hash table O(1) per insert. Top-10: iterate O(n) then sort O(n log n).

concepts.title: Insertion and Merge Sort
concepts.explanation: Insertion sort: O(n²) worst, O(n) best, O(1) space, stable. Merge sort: T(n)=2T(n/2)+O(n) → O(n log n) all cases, O(n) space, stable.

concepts.title: BFS and DFS
concepts.explanation: BFS: queue, level-by-level, shortest path in unweighted graphs, O(V+E). DFS: stack/recursion, depth-first, O(V+E). BFS: shortest path. DFS: cycle detection, topological sort.
concepts.worked_example: Shortest unweighted path: BFS from source, track parent pointers, trace back on reaching destination. BFS guarantees minimum hops.
